page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. It features 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core, the instance utilizes dedicated CPU allocation. The processor architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations.

Performance benchmarks place the instance consistently in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1836.0 and a multi-core score of 10971.0, with a PassMark CPU score of 21396.12. Notably, the server demonstrates strong performance in text processing, scoring 2375.0 on Geekbench, and in lightweight LLM text generation, processing 262.3 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model. Memory bandwidth is measured at 115.64 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 66.19 GB/sec for large uncached writes.

The instance does not include bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning external storage and networking resources must be provisioned separately. This hardware profile is cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not require local NVMe SSDs or dedicated GPU acceleration. It is suited for workloads such as database operations, in-memory caching via Redis, software compilation, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 8 physical cores and dedicated CPU allocation, it includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show average performance across most compute and memory tests, with strong results in text processing and lightweight LLM text generation. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-heavy workloads. It is suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and Intel x86_64 architecture, delivering balanced performance for database and in-memory workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive workloads.
